text:
Market today: Risk-off tone. Kashkari floating a rate hike is the real driver - higher rates compress tech multiples and make margin borrowing more expensive. Micron sinking adds chip-sector pressure right where your book lives.

Your holdings: AVGO is the alarm - your largest position, down 15%, and chip sentiment is deteriorating today. PLTR is down nearly 13% with no cushion. SPAL is down 13% and it's a 2x leveraged ETF - decay is eating you alive on top of the move. Remember, margin amplifies every one of these drops. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO - treat it as a live grenade until it finds footing.

Watch: (1) How far Micron's selloff bleeds into AVGO and SMH - if SMH breaks toward 580, your whole chip sleeve is in trouble. (2) Any Fed speakers doubling down on Kashkari's rate-hike tone - that's the toggle for the whole tech trade.

Bottom line: Your biggest risk today is sitting in three simultaneous losers on margin - AVGO, PLTR, and SPAL all need a hard look before end of day.
